title: AWS IAM Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on IAM policies, roles, users, and security principles.

questions.question: What is the Principle of Least Privilege?

options.text: Granting everyone AdministratorAccess so they can do their jobs without asking for permission.
Granting only the exact, minimum permissions necessary to perform a specific task.
Using IAM Roles only for third-party access.
Avoiding the use of Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).

questions.question: Which AWS IAM component is designed specifically to be assumed by an AWS service (like an EC2 instance) rather than being assigned long-term static credentials?

options.text: IAM User
IAM Policy
IAM Group
IAM Role

questions.question: In the context of an IAM Policy JSON document, what does the 'Resource' field specify?

options.text: The type of EC2 instance to launch.
The specific AWS entity (like a specific S3 bucket ARN) that the actions apply to.
The user attempting to make the API call.
The encryption key used to protect the data.

questions.question: If an IAM user has no policies attached to them, what access do they have by default?

options.text: Read-Only access to all services.
Administrator access.
Implicit Deny to all services.
They can only access the billing console.

questions.question: Why is it dangerous to hardcode AWS Access Keys into your application's source code?

options.text: Access keys expire after 1 hour automatically.
Compiling the code with keys breaks the compiler.
If the source code is pushed to a public repository like GitHub, bots will steal the keys and mine cryptocurrency on your account within minutes.
AWS charges extra for hardcoded keys.
